title: Playbook - Microsoft Graph SharePoint Drive And File CRUD
applies_to: Upload, list, update, move, download, and delete SharePoint files and folders through Microsoft Graph drives
prerequisites: Resolved site ID and drive ID or enough information to discover them
Target file path, folder path, parent item ID, or driveItem ID
required_permissions: Verify least-privilege file permissions such as Files.ReadWrite or Sites.ReadWrite.All for the exact read or write pattern
For application permissions, ensure admin consent is in place before unattended uploads or deletions
request_sequence: Resolve the site if needed: GET /sites/{hostname}:/{relative-path} or another site-discovery method.
List drives under the site: GET /sites/{site-id}/drives or use /sites/{site-id}/drive for the default document library.
List files and folders in a location: GET /drives/{drive-id}/items/{item-id}/children or by path under the drive.
Create a folder: POST /drives/{drive-id}/items/{parent-id}/children with a driveItem body that includes a folder facet.
Large upload: POST /drives/{drive-id}/items/{parent-id}:/{filename}:/createUploadSession, then upload chunks to the returned session URL.
Download a file: GET /drives/{drive-id}/items/{item-id}/content or by path.
Update file or folder metadata, or move an item: PATCH /drives/{drive-id}/items/{item-id}.
Delete a file or folder: DELETE /drives/{drive-id}/items/{item-id}.

confirmed_facts.fact: Document libraries are represented as drives under the site.
Listing children of a folder or container driveItem is supported through the children relationship.
Creating a child driveItem with a folder facet under the children collection creates a folder.
File download uses the driveItem content endpoint.
Small file uploads can be sent directly with PUT to the content endpoint, and this method supports files up to 250 MB.
Large file uploads should use createUploadSession and chunked transfer.
Updating driveItem properties can also move an item by changing its parentReference.
Deleting a driveItem moves it to the recycle bin and returns 204 No Content on success.

implementation_notes: Use upload sessions by default when file size may exceed simple upload limits or when transfers need resiliency.
Treat drive IDs and driveItem IDs as stable handles after discovery.
Preserve returned metadata from upload and folder-creation responses so later steps can reference item IDs without extra lookups.
Use PATCH for metadata changes, rename operations, or moves; use PUT content only for actual file content writes.
common_failures: 403 from missing file or site permissions.
409 naming collisions when creating folders or uploading to an existing path without the intended conflict behavior.
Interrupted upload-session chunking if the client does not honor the upload-session protocol.
Deleting by the wrong item ID or path and unexpectedly recycling the wrong document library item.
